evidence: `src/domains/generation/types/generationRow.ts` defines `GenerationRow` as `PersistedGenerationRow & GenerationViewModel` and labels it a migration compatibility alias, coupling DB/API shape and UI shape in one exported type.

`src/domains/generation/types/shot.ts` repeats the same boundary merge pattern with `Shot = PersistedShotRow & ShotViewModel`, then embeds `GenerationRow[]` in `ShotViewModel.images`, propagating blended contracts across domain types.

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` centralizes a global singleton (`clientInstance`) plus one-time instrumentation install, so any caller of `getSupabaseClient()` implicitly triggers cross-cutting runtime setup.

Multiple files carry explicit 'compatibility alias during migration' (`handleError` alias in `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts`, `GenerationRow` alias, `Shot` alias), indicating temporary bridge types/entrypoints are still part of core public surfaces.
Type ownership is blurred: persistence concerns (`Persisted*`) and UI interaction concerns (`*ViewModel`) are co-located and re-exported as merged canonical types in domain-level files.

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` mixes client access, singleton lifecycle, and instrumentation/bootstrap orchestration in one shared module, increasing the number of responsibilities at a high-fan-in entrypoint.

Task-construction modules manually rebuild overlapping orchestration payload shapes (`orchestration_contract`, segment mode, lora/phase settings, lineage ids) in multiple places rather than sharing a canonical builder: `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`.

`src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts` exports a method-forwarding facade (`dataFreshnessManager`) over a singleton while also exporting direct instance access, adding indirection without interface reduction.

`src/integrations/supabase/instrumentation/realtime/index.ts` exposes `installRealtimeInstrumentation` as a pure pass-through to `installRealtimeInstrumentationImpl`, with no policy/translation boundary.

Lightbox handoff seams are high-entropy: `VideoLightbox` assembles mode/env/shared/edit/render models and forwards deep nested props into `LightboxLayout`; the render model builds large cross-module wiring objects (`shotWorkflow`, `actions`, button groups) in `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx`.

Equivalent seam wiring is duplicated between image and video paths (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx` and `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx`), increasing drift risk at integration boundaries.

`src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx` builds a very large `JoinClipsSettingsForm` prop bag with many one-field adapter lambdas, so UI seam logic is spread across caller and form API instead of a tighter boundary.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts` contains dense fallback-heavy internals (`buildSegmentState`, `buildTaskParamsPayload`, `buildOrchestratorDetails`) that repeatedly merge values from `params`, `orig`, and `orchDetails`, making branch intent hard to verify and maintain.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` has large memo/dependency surfaces and many condition-derived flags (`showPanel`, `needsFullscreenLayout`, `needsTasksPaneOffset`) mixed with action wiring, reducing local readability.

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts` combines callback adaptation, deletion orchestration, cache mutation, and invalidation in one hook, producing multi-responsibility flow at function level.
`useCredits` API includes command methods and a nested hook factory (`useCreditLedger`) rather than a flat hook contract used by sibling feature hooks.

Auth-related data functions in one module throw strings like 'Authentication required', while others emit context-rich helper-generated errors, creating mixed failure contracts for similar operations.
Route loader auth check (`authRedirectLoader`) returns redirect/null semantics that differ from authenticated feature hooks relying on exceptions and mutations.

src/tools/join-clips/utils/validation.ts contains extensive explanatory comments and duplicated narrative around the same formula (lines 1-17, 36-42, 135-142), increasing noise relative to logic size.

supabase/functions/_shared/storagePaths.ts and supabase/functions/_shared/systemLogger.ts both include long tutorial-style header comments and per-method restatement comments that mostly repeat code intent instead of explaining non-obvious constraints.
src/shared/lib/supabaseSession.ts has heavy operational commentary (lock contention discussion) while the implementation is small, indicating documentation bloat concentrated in utility code.
`GenerationRowDto` carries legacy aliases (`thumbnail_url`, `shot_generation_id`, `shotImageEntryId`) while `GenerationRow` remains a compatibility intersection type.

Comments explicitly mark compatibility during migration but active exported surfaces still depend on the compatibility type (`src/domains/generation/types/generationRow.ts`, `src/domains/generation/types/generationRowDto.ts`).
Transport exports both modern and legacy-facing types together (`src/domains/generation/types/transport.ts`), signaling unresolved boundary cleanup.

`src/pages/Home/components/` has `file_count: 26` (>10 threshold) with mixed concerns (UI panes, modal, video component, selector, and a hook file), indicating flat-directory overload per structure profile.
`src/pages/Home/components/` total LOC is 2625 with low cohesion signals (avg fan-out 1.8, imported by only `src/pages/Home/`), suggesting internal sub-packaging is overdue.

`src/pages/Home/components/installInstructions/` and `src/pages/Home/components/motionComparison/` already exist as subfolders; remaining peers still crowd the top-level component directory instead of following that decomposition.
`config/testing/` includes mixed zoning (`zones: { config: 1, production: 2 }`) despite being test-config concern, indicating organizational drift.

Multiple controller/orchestrator hooks use very large parameter and return surfaces that mostly relay state/handlers (e.g., useImageEditOrchestrator has a broad composed input object and builds a large context object in one place with a massive dependency list, src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useImageEditOrchestrator.ts:32 and :394-526).

Lightbox-level composition repeatedly re-groups and forwards flat props without much local behavior (useBundledLightboxProps in src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/MediaLightbox.tsx:80-147 and workflow prop composition in src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useLightboxWorkflowProps.ts:80-149).

Shot editor controller hooks aggregate unrelated concerns (audio, structure video, naming, join configuration, generation settings) into broad facades with large pass-through APIs (src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/controllers/useEditingController.ts:24-225 and useGenerationController.ts:7-235).

complete_task server handlers blend orchestration, persistence, migration/self-healing, and heavy debug logging in the same module (supabase/functions/complete_task/generation-child.ts:41-540), while params.ts mixes path extraction, payload mutation, MIME mapping, and generation param construction in one utility file (supabase/functions/complete_task/params.ts:15-353).

Export style is mixed within closely related UI/page surfaces: `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/index.tsx` exports both named and default (`export { MediaGallery }; export default MediaGallery;`), while many sibling shared UI modules use named-only exports (for example `src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx`).
Route/page modules also vary (`src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx` default export only) versus hook/component areas preferring named exports.

Error context naming convention is inconsistent in auth hooks: `src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts` logs with generic `context: 'HomePage'` while nearby auth/storage flows use scoped contexts like `useAuthReferralFinalize.*` and call-site-provided context strings.

Canonical runtime error path exists (`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts`), but several paths bypass it and use direct `console.error` in production/runtime code (`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts`).

Mixed error contracts in task/generation data access: some APIs throw (`resolveGenerationProjectId` in `src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts`), while adjacent APIs return `OperationResult` failures (`getPrimaryTaskIdForGenerationResult`, `getPrimaryTaskIdsForGenerationsResult`), forcing callers to handle multiple paradigms.

Mutation layer mixes throw-and-bubble with side-effect reporting (`src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` throws in mutationFns but reports in `onError`), while other flows log and return early (`src/pages/Home/hooks/auth/useAuthReferralFinalize.ts`).

Project scoping is optional in generation repository helpers (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts` returns early when `expectedProjectId` is missing; generation/task queries only add `.eq('project_id', ...)` when option provided).

Mutation coverage is uneven: generation mutations can apply project checks when `projectId` is passed (`useDeleteGeneration`, `useUpdateGenerationLocation`, `useToggleGenerationStar`), but variant deletion path (`useDeleteVariant` via `useDeleteFromTable`) deletes by `id` only with no project scope in `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`.

Edge-function auth posture is not uniform across reviewed files: `supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts` enforces `authenticateRequest` and service-role checks, while reviewed `complete_task` modules operate on privileged inputs/service key parameters without local actor validation boundaries.

findings.summary: Domain types merge persistence and UI models, creating boundary leakage across modules.
evidence: `GenerationRow` is an intersection of `PersistedGenerationRow` and `GenerationViewModel` and exported as a compatibility alias.
`Shot` is an intersection of `PersistedShotRow` and `ShotViewModel`, and `ShotViewModel.images` depends on `GenerationRow[]`, cascading mixed contracts.
These types are declared in domain-level files, so callers can consume UI-only and persistence-only fields through one shared contract.
findings.suggestion: Split exports into explicit boundary types only (`Persisted*`, `*ViewModel`) and introduce dedicated mappers at feature boundaries. Deprecate and remove `GenerationRow`/`Shot` aliases in a staged migration so cross-module consumers must choose the correct contract.

findings.summary: Compatibility aliases remain on core entrypoints, obscuring final ownership boundaries.
evidence: `handleError` is kept as a backward-compatible alias to `reportRuntimeError` in a high-fan-in error entrypoint.
`GenerationRow` and `Shot` are both documented as compatibility aliases during migration yet still exported from canonical domain type modules.
Keeping transitional names on primary modules extends dual-path usage and makes intended stable APIs less explicit.
findings.suggestion: Create a migration cutoff: move compatibility aliases into a dedicated `legacy` module, keep canonical exports alias-free on primary modules, and update imports incrementally with lint/enforcement to prevent new alias usage.

findings.summary: Multiple task modules independently rebuild near-identical orchestration payload contracts, creating abstraction drift risk.
evidence: `buildJoinClipsPayload`, `buildIndividualTravelSegmentParams`/`buildTaskParamsPayload`, and `buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload` all hand-assemble large payload objects with overlapping concerns (lineage ids, orchestration contract, mode flags, lora/phase settings).
`individualTravelSegment` constructs orchestration contract in more than one payload layer (`orchestrator_details` and top-level), reinforcing schema duplication pressure.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared typed orchestration payload assembler (core contract + reusable field mappers for lineage, motion, loras, resolution, segment metadata) and keep task-specific modules limited to delta fields.

findings.summary: Several exported abstractions are pure forwarding layers that add indirection without behavior.
evidence: `dataFreshnessManager` re-exports nearly every method as `(...args) => getOrCreateDataFreshnessManager().method(...args)` while `getDataFreshnessManager` already exists.
`installRealtimeInstrumentation` only calls `installRealtimeInstrumentationImpl` with identical signature/semantics.
findings.suggestion: Collapse or narrow forwarding layers: export one canonical API per subsystem (either direct singleton accessor or facade, not both) and keep wrappers only when they enforce policy or adaptation.

findings.summary: Lightbox integration seams duplicate orchestration mappings across image/video paths and rely on oversized cross-module prop wiring.
evidence: Both image and video flows build very similar `shotWorkflow` and `actions` handoff objects before calling shared workflow/layout helpers.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` combines layout decisions, task panel policy, download/delete/apply action adaptation, and workflow props assembly into one seam function.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared seam composer for common workflow/action mappings and keep media-specific render models focused on mode-specific deltas.

findings.summary: Join Clips page-to-form boundary uses a large adapter object with many field-level setter wrappers, indicating interface mismatch.
evidence: `JoinClipsPageLayout` constructs `clipSettings`, `motionConfig`, and `uiState` with numerous `setX: (value) => joinSettings.updateField(...)` lambdas.
A substantial part of container code is dedicated to field-by-field translation rather than orchestration logic.
findings.suggestion: Move to a narrower form contract (e.g., `settings` + `updateField(s)` + typed selectors) or expose a form-specific controller hook to remove repetitive adapter lambdas.

findings.summary: Core builders rely on long fallback chains and mixed responsibilities, reducing local reasoning clarity.
evidence: `buildSegmentState` and related builders repeatedly merge values from `params`, `orig`, and `orchDetails` across many fields, with coupled defaults and mode-specific branches.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` interleaves UI policy flags, callback adapters, and large memo dependency lists in a single function.
`useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` mixes async DB operations, deletion policies, optimistic cache edits, and query invalidation inside one hook.
findings.suggestion: Split by intent: isolate normalization/default-resolution helpers from payload assembly and separate action orchestration from render-state calculation to keep each function single-purpose.

findings.summary: Edge error serialization uses a field name that does not exist on the shared failure contract.
evidence: `EdgeFailureResult` defines `errorCode` as the canonical identifier field.
`toErrorResponse` serializes `error: result.code`, so callers can receive missing/undefined error identifiers despite `edgeFailure(...)` providing `errorCode`.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one field name across the subsystem (prefer `errorCode` since producers already emit it), update `toErrorResponse` to read that field, and add a regression test covering strict JSON parse failures to assert the response payload includes the expected error code.

findings.summary: Boundary layers convert `OperationResult` into throws, then upstream seams re-handle errors ad hoc.
evidence: Repository APIs return `OperationResult`, but query wrappers immediately `throw result.error` on `!ok`.
A higher layer (`preloadGenerationTaskMappings`) catches those throws and continues batch processing, while other call paths may rely on thrown behavior directly.
Join-clips metadata repeats the same dual API (`loadClipDurationResult` and `loadClipDuration`), indicating repeated seam translation logic instead of a single failure contract.
findings.suggestion: Choose one failure protocol per boundary (prefer `OperationResult` at shared library seams), keep wrappers consistent with that choice, and push throw-conversion only to UI/controller edges that need exception flow.

findings.summary: Shared edge request utilities expose multiple incompatible return contracts for similar boundary concerns.
evidence: `parseJsonBody` returns `EdgeOperationResult<Record<string, unknown>>`, but `parseJsonBodyStrict` wraps this into `{ body?, errorResponse? }` and `ensureRequestMethod` uses `Response | null`.
`checkRateLimit` returns custom `RateLimitResult` while response building is split into `rateLimitResponse`, diverging from `EdgeOperationResult`-style handling used elsewhere.
findings.suggestion: Unify shared edge utilities around one envelope type (e.g., `EdgeOperationResult<T>` plus a single response adapter), and deprecate mixed ad-hoc wrappers (`Response | null`, custom `{ body?, errorResponse? }`) to reduce per-handler branching and glue code.

findings.summary: Error handling migration is incomplete because legacy and new entrypoints are both active.
evidence: handleError.ts line 41 keeps `handleError` as backward-compatible alias to `reportRuntimeError`.
supabaseSession.ts imports `handleError` and calls it in the catch path instead of using `reportRuntimeError`.
findings.suggestion: Migrate remaining callers to `reportRuntimeError`, then remove the alias export in a single cleanup pass with a codemod/search-replace plus typecheck.

findings.summary: Join-clips validation combines global and positional frame rules inconsistently, risking over-rejection.
evidence: getMinFramesRequired() returns positional breakdown but first/last `total` is set to global `minRequired` (`gap + 2*context`), not positional sum.
validateClipsForJoin() validates every clip with `clip.frameCount >= required.total`, so first/last clips can be rejected even when positional requirements are satisfied.
findings.suggestion: Choose one canonical rule for validity (global per-clip minimum or positional requirements), encode it once, and keep positional values for visualization only.

findings.summary: Multiple utility files contain tutorial-style comment volume that obscures core logic.
evidence: validation.ts repeats the same REPLACE/INSERT constraint explanations across file header, helper docs, and validator docs.
storagePaths.ts and systemLogger.ts include long prose headers plus line-by-line intent comments for straightforward operations.
findings.suggestion: Condense comments to non-obvious invariants and edge cases; remove repetitive restatements and move long operational notes to dedicated docs if needed.

findings.summary: SystemLogger’s console formatting can throw on circular context, making logging itself fragile.
evidence: Each log method calls `JSON.stringify(context)` directly before buffering.
No guard/safe serializer exists around stringify, so circular metadata can throw before `addToBuffer` runs.
findings.suggestion: Add a safe serialization helper (try/catch with fallback token) and reuse it across all log methods to guarantee logging path stability.

findings.summary: Root-level config files are peripheral stragglers despite config-domain affinity
evidence: Packet root_files: `tailwind.config.ts` fan_in 0, `vite.config.ts` fan_in 0 (both peripheral).
`tailwind.config.ts` imports only `./config/tailwind/theme/*`; `vite.config.ts` imports only `./config/vite/policy`.
These files are low-fan-in entrypoints colocated away from their owning config domains.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) create `config/tailwind/tailwind.config.ts` and `config/vite/vite.config.ts` as canonical configs; 2) keep thin root bridge files temporarily that re-export from new locations to avoid breaking tooling; 3) update scripts/tooling to point directly to new paths, then remove root bridges. Import update/validation commands: `rg -n "tailwind.config|vite.config" .`, `git mv tailwind.config.ts config/tailwind/tailwind.config.ts`, `git mv vite.config.ts config/vite/vite.config.ts`, `npm run build` (or `pnpm build`), `npm run test` (or `pnpm test`).

findings.summary: Shared components package mixes unrelated responsibilities, blurring ownership boundaries
evidence: `AuthGate.tsx` is an app/session gating concern, while `BulkEditControls.tsx` is prompt-edit workflow UI; both live in the same broad shared bucket.
`ChunkLoadErrorBoundary.test.tsx` sits at the same top-level component directory, reinforcing a flat mixed-concern package shape.
Packet directory profile shows `src/shared/components/` has 69 files with broad inbound/outbound coupling, indicating weak capability partitioning.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) create capability folders (`src/shared/components/auth/`, `src/shared/components/prompt-editing/`, `src/shared/components/error-boundaries/` and matching `__tests__` mirrors); 2) move `AuthGate.tsx`, `BulkEditControls.tsx`, and `ChunkLoadErrorBoundary*` into those folders first; 3) add short-lived barrel exports in `src/shared/components/index.ts` for compatibility, then migrate imports and remove deprecated barrels. Import update/validation commands: `rg -n "@/shared/components/(AuthGate|BulkEditControls|ChunkLoadErrorBoundary)" src`, `git mv src/shared/components/AuthGate.tsx src/shared/components/auth/AuthGate.tsx`, `git mv src/shared/components/BulkEditControls.tsx src/shared/components/prompt-editing/BulkEditControls.tsx`, `git mv src/shared/components/ChunkLoadErrorBoundary.test.tsx src/shared/components/error-boundaries/__tests__/ChunkLoadErrorBoundary.test.tsx`, `npm run typecheck` (or `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit`), `npm run test` (or `pnpm test`).

findings.summary: Compatibility aliases keep old and new contracts active, weakening boundary enforcement.
evidence: README.md line 49 states boundary ownership around explicit generation types, but generationRow.ts lines 40-41 still expose the legacy merged alias `GenerationRow`.
README.md frames `handleError` runtime contract (line 44), yet handleError.ts line 42 still exports legacy `handleError` alias in parallel with `reportRuntimeError`.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a dated compatibility policy: Stage 1 add `@deprecated` JSDoc on aliases (`handleError`, `GenerationRow`) and publish sunset criteria in README; Stage 2 update imports to canonical symbols with `rg "\bhandleError\b|\bGenerationRow\b" src` and replace in callers; Stage 3 remove aliases once import count reaches zero and validate with `npm run typecheck` and `npm run test`.

findings.summary: Architecture contracts are duplicated across README and code comments, inviting drift.
evidence: README.md lines 42-49 documents runtime contracts with implementation-level specifics.
client.ts lines 36-41 and handleError.ts lines 5-13 restate canonical contract language in code, creating multiple normative sources.
findings.suggestion: Split governance from implementation notes: keep README with short policy bullets and a single link to a dedicated `docs/runtime-contracts.md`; make code comments local/behavioral only; add a small CI docs check (`rg "Canonical|Contract:" README.md src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`) to flag duplicated normative statements.

findings.summary: The `shared/components/ui` package is overloaded and mixes unrelated responsibilities.
evidence: holistic structure shows `src/shared/components/ui/` has 56 files, exceeding the >10 mixed-concern overload signal.

Coupling matrix and directory profile show high inbound affinity from many directories (107 from `src/shared/components/`, 59 from travel-between-images components), indicating a broad hub with weak internal boundaries.
`button.tsx` (component primitive) and `cn.ts` (generic styling utility) are co-located despite different ownership concerns.
findings.suggestion: Run a staged reorg: Stage 1 create target folders `src/shared/ui/primitives/`, `src/shared/ui/foundation/`, and `src/shared/ui/feedback/`; Stage 2 move low-risk utilities first (`cn.ts`, `Slot.tsx`) into `foundation`; Stage 3 move primitives (`button.tsx`, `input.tsx`, `label.tsx`) into `primitives`; Stage 4 move toast/tooltip/sonner infrastructure into `feedback`; Stage 5 update imports with codemods/grep (`rg "@/shared/components/ui/" src -l`) and path rewrites; Stage 6 validate with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test`, and a focused smoke pass on core pages.

findings.summary: Lightbox orchestration relies on large pass-through prop bundles that dilute ownership boundaries.
evidence: useImageEditOrchestrator takes a large composite props object and assembles a very broad ImageEditState object with dozens of forwarded members and dependencies (useImageEditOrchestrator.ts:32-92, :394-526).
MediaLightbox introduces useBundledLightboxProps, which mostly remaps flat props into grouped objects and forwards them to ImageLightbox/VideoLightbox (MediaLightbox.tsx:80-147, :155-229).
useLightboxWorkflowProps similarly re-composes workflow props via nested input groups and memoized forwarding without substantial transformation (useLightboxWorkflowProps.ts:59-149).
findings.suggestion: Define explicit feature-scoped view models (e.g., `shotActions`, `editModeState`, `workflowControls`) produced by their owning hooks, and let top-level components compose 3-5 stable domain objects instead of forwarding individual fields. Remove pass-through layers that only rename/regroup data.

findings.summary: ShotEditor controllers combine unrelated domains into god-facade hooks.
evidence: useEditingController merges structure video, audio, name editing, join settings, and join execution and then returns a very broad mixed API (useEditingController.ts:39-225).

useGenerationController accepts a very large mixed parameter surface and forwards most fields into downstream generation hooks, making boundary intent hard to reason about (useGenerationController.ts:7-125, :155-220).
findings.suggestion: Split controller responsibilities by workflow (`useShotMetadataController`, `useJoinController`, `useBatchGenerationController`) and pass typed config objects per workflow. Keep each controller focused on one mutation lifecycle and expose a narrower return contract.

findings.summary: Image generation submission logic is fragmented across wrappers with duplicated workflow steps.
evidence: useFormSubmission and useSubmissionHandlers mostly shuttle large prop groups between layers before reaching behavior hooks (useFormSubmission.ts:20-109, useSubmissionHandlers.ts:24-86).

useSubmitHandler and usePromptQueueHandlers duplicate orchestration steps (trigger automated button, runIncomingTask wrapper, task param building, onGenerate execution), creating parallel implementations of near-identical flow control (useSubmitHandler.ts:71-113 and :122-135; usePromptQueueHandlers.ts:64-77 and :100-134).
findings.suggestion: Consolidate submission execution into one reusable command helper (`runSubmissionScenario`) that handles shared side effects and task execution; make mode handlers provide only scenario-specific inputs (prompt source, expected count, task param strategy).

findings.summary: complete_task generation handlers mix orchestration, DB persistence, migration repair, and diagnostics in single modules.
evidence: generation-child.ts contains control flow for child creation strategy, variant fallback logic, FK validation/backfill, record insertion, and verbose segment debug logging in one file/function cluster (generation-child.ts:41-540).

params.ts combines generic extraction framework, per-field extraction policy, payload mutation (`setThumbnailInParams`), content-type mapping, and generation param shaping, increasing conceptual spread in one utility module (params.ts:42-353).
findings.suggestion: Refactor complete_task into explicit stages (`normalizeParams`, `resolveTargetGeneration`, `persistGeneration`, `persistVariant`, `diagnostics`) with narrow module boundaries; move debug/reporting helpers and migration-healing logic behind dedicated services so core completion flow stays linear.

findings.summary: Generation and variant mutation paths do not enforce a uniform project-bound authorization scope.
evidence: `resolveGenerationProjectId` returns `null` when `expectedProjectId` is absent, so no project equality check is applied (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts`).
`useDeleteVariant` deletes from `generation_variants` with `.eq('id', id)` only, unlike generation delete/update paths that can include project scoping (`src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`).
Task-id lookup helpers only apply `.eq('project_id', ...)` when caller supplies it, making authorization behavior caller-dependent (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts`).
findings.suggestion: Make project scoping mandatory for generation/variant mutations and lookup helpers (accept required `projectId`, enforce `.eq('project_id', projectId)` in all mutation/query paths, and reject calls missing scope). For variant deletes, join/resolve parent generation project before delete or add DB-side constrained RPC.

findings.summary: Error handling is fragmented between canonical handlers, direct console logging, thrown exceptions, and result-return APIs.
evidence: Canonical `reportRuntimeError/handleError` exists as the standardized runtime entrypoint (`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts`).

Complete-task modules directly emit `console.error` in catch/degraded paths instead of a shared error envelope (`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts`).
Repository APIs mix `throw` and `OperationResult` failure returns in the same module, creating inconsistent caller obligations (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts`).
findings.suggestion: Define per-layer error contract boundaries and enforce one pattern per boundary: e.g., edge functions return structured failure objects/log via one logger utility; client repositories either throw consistently or return `OperationResult` consistently (not both).

findings.summary: Module conventions drift in export style and error context naming, reducing predictability across sibling code.
evidence: `MediaGallery` exports both named and default in one module, while nearby shared UI modules use named-only (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/index.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx`).
`JoinClipsPage` uses default export only (`src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx`), contributing to mixed import conventions across related surfaces.
Error context strings in auth hooks are inconsistent: generic `HomePage` in `useDiscordSignIn` vs scoped `useAuthReferralFinalize.*` in referral finalize flow.
findings.suggestion: Adopt and enforce one export convention per layer (prefer named-only for shared/component-hook modules) and standardize error context format (e.g., `feature.hook.operation`) via lint rule or helper constants.

findings.summary: Billing auth/data errors use ad-hoc throws and console logging instead of the shared contextual auth+error pipeline used by sibling hooks.
evidence: `useCredits` throws plain `new Error('Authentication required')` and `new Error('Failed to ...')` in multiple paths and logs with `console.error` (`useCredits.ts` lines 56, 66, 82, 93, 106, 122).
`useApiTokens` and `useApiKeys` rely on `requireSession/requireUserFromSession` and route failures to `handleError`, preserving context consistency.
`ensureAuthenticatedSession.ts` already provides standardized contextual auth errors but is not used in `useCredits`.
findings.suggestion: Refactor `useCredits` to call `requireSession/requireUserFromSession` for auth checks, replace inline `throw new Error(...)` wrapping with shared contextual error construction, and remove direct `console.error` in favor of `handleError` with `context` + `logData`.

findings.summary: `useCredits` exposes a nested hook factory (`useCreditLedger`) which creates an atypical and fragile API compared with peer feature hooks.
evidence: `useCredits` defines and returns `useCreditLedger` (lines 144-151, 248), meaning consumers must invoke a hook returned by another hook.
Peer hooks (`useApiTokens`, `useApiKeys`) expose stable data/actions directly, not returned hook factories.
This API shape increases risk of conditional invocation patterns and inconsistent call semantics across feature hooks.
findings.suggestion: Split ledger into `useCreditLedger(params)` as a top-level hook and keep `useCredits` focused on balance + mutations, then update call sites to use both hooks explicitly for predictable hook ordering.

findings.summary: Generation row typing still mixes legacy transport aliases, persisted schema, and UI view fields in exported compatibility contracts.
evidence: `GenerationRow` is still `PersistedGenerationRow & GenerationViewModel` with comment 'Compatibility alias during migration'.
`GenerationRowDto` includes legacy alias fields (`thumbnail_url`, `shot_generation_id`, `shotImageEntryId`) as active type surface.
`transport.ts` re-exports legacy DTO shape next to modern persisted params, keeping old/new contracts coupled.
findings.suggestion: Stage migration by introducing distinct `GenerationDomainModel` and `GenerationTransportDto`, map legacy aliases only in dedicated boundary mappers, deprecate `GenerationRow` intersection, then remove legacy alias fields after importer updates.

findings.summary: Home component package is overloaded with mixed concerns in a flat folder, reducing navigability and ownership clarity.
evidence: Structure profile shows `src/pages/Home/components/` has `file_count: 26` (>10 threshold) and includes both UI components and behavior hook files.
Existing subfolders (`installInstructions/`, `motionComparison/`) indicate partial decomposition, but most peers remain in one large flat package.
Large single files inside this folder (`HeroSection.tsx` 381 LOC, `TravelSelector.tsx` 279 LOC) amplify discoverability and cohesion problems.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create target folders `src/pages/Home/components/hero`, `.../panes`, `.../selectors`, `.../media`, `.../modals`, `.../hooks`; (2) move files in order: first leaf utility/hook files (`useTravelAutoAdvance.ts`, modal/install assets), then selector/media components, then pane components, then `HeroSection`; (3) run import updates and checks with `rg "from './components/" src/pages/Home src/app`, `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit`, and `pnpm -s vitest src/pages/Home --run` to validate path and behavior integrity.

findings.summary: Supabase runtime initialization relies on global browser mutation and dynamic subsystem wiring that increases startup blast radius.
evidence: `initializeSupabaseRuntime` always wires auth manager, realtime instrumentation, reconnect scheduler, and optional auto-login in one call path.
Window instrumentation overrides `window.onerror`, `window.onunhandledrejection`, and `window.WebSocket` when enabled.
Auth manager triggers reconnect scheduling through runtime dynamic import, coupling auth events to reconnect infrastructure.
findings.suggestion: Split runtime bootstrap into explicit phases (`core client`, `auth wiring`, `diagnostics instrumentation`) with environment-scoped entry points, and isolate browser-global patches behind a dedicated diagnostics initializer that is never loaded in normal production bootstrap.
